title: Synagogue Attack Tests Bonds of Michigan Community - The New York Times - The New York Times
summary: A terror attack at a Michigan synagogue (and related incidents at a Virginia university) has rattled local communities, testing bonds among residents, prompting law enforcement and civic responses, and raising concerns about safety and interfaith relations.

title: Oklahoma AG: State board rigged Jewish charter school rejection
summary: Oklahoma Attorney General Gentner Drummond filed a motion asking a district judge to intervene, alleging state board officials rigged a vote to reject a proposed Jewish charter school. The filing accuses officials of manipulating the approval process and seeks judicial review of the board's decision.

title: Jewish school bombed in Amsterdam as antisemitic violence rocks the country
summary: A blast struck the outer wall of Cheider, the only Orthodox Jewish school in the Netherlands in Amsterdam’s Buitenveldert, as authorities investigate amid a broader surge of antisemitic incidents across the country.

title: Explosion at Amsterdam Jewish school was 'deliberate attack', mayor says
summary: An explosion caused minor damage to a Jewish school in an upscale Amsterdam neighborhood; the city's mayor described it as a deliberate attack on the Jewish community and authorities are investigating, with no major injuries reported.

title: Reactions from the hometown of man who attacked the synagogue in Michigan
summary: An NPR report profiles reactions in the hometown of a man who attacked a Michigan synagogue, noting he had lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon and recording relatives’ and neighbors’ perspectives on his actions and motives.

title: In a small Lebanese town, grief and fear follow the Michigan synagogue attack
summary: After a suspected attacker targeted a Michigan synagogue, reporting from his small Lebanese hometown — where he lost family in an Israeli strike — describes local grief, fear, and mixed reactions as residents reckon with the attack and its cross-border motivations.

title: Israel says antisemitism raging in Netherlands after Jewish school blast
summary: After an overnight blast outside a Jewish school in Amsterdam, Israel's foreign ministry warned an "antisemitism epidemic is raging" in the Netherlands, urging the Dutch government to take stronger action; the incident raised concerns about the safety of the Jewish community and prompted international condemnation.

title: Taha group pleas for $676K grant restoration
summary: The Dandenong-based Taha Association Centre is seeking reinstatement of a roughly $670,000 federal grant that was cancelled after the centre publicly mourned the death of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; the grant was intended for facility upgrades.

title: Security guard injured in Michigan synagogue car ramming ID’d as longtime police officer - New York Post
summary: A security guard was injured when a vehicle rammed into a Michigan synagogue; the guard has been identified as a longtime police officer and authorities are investigating the incident as they assess motives and community impact.

title: MS NOW Host Rips Trump and Hegseth for Unleashing a ‘Bit of Racism’ on Iran — Which Is Merely an ‘Opponent’
summary: MS NOW host Antonia Hylton criticized Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth for employing racialized language about Iran and Iranians, calling it a disturbing example of racism rather than legitimate political critique.
